observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to restore the book value of the derecognized PPE accounts to strictly comply with the guidelines and procedures set forth in the COA Circular 2020-06 on the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs; and
Direct the Inventory Committee/GSO to inspect the fully depreciated assets and to prepare the Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP), if warranted and to provide the Municipal Accountant with supporting documents as evidence of disposal for proper derecognition from the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Supply Officer to furnish the Municipal Accountant the Property Transfer Report (PTR) issued by the Provincial Local Government Unit, DOH, and PCSO as a basis of recording the asset; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the books of account by debiting Motor Vehicles, crediting Income from Grants and Donation and to compute for the corresponding depreciation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the GSO and RHU to coordinate for proper inventory-taking and reporting of the transferred drugs and medicines from DOH; and
Require the GSO to furnish the PTRS and Acceptance and Inspection Report to the Municipal Accountant to serve as basis in recording the transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries and follow relevant accounting guidelines for affected accounts to preclude inaccuracy and unreliability of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to bring the Inventory Held for Consumption, distribution, and Food Supplies Expenses to their correct balances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to explain the reasons for the unresolved reconciling items, furnish this Office with an update on the status of the correspondence sent to LBP, and provide a summary of the annual actions and initiatives undertaken by their office since these reconciling items were identified in the BRS.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components;
Require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and their components, including the Road Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General Services Officer at the end of accounting period; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare for the Road Network Ledger Cards; to prepare a complete disclosure of road network system in the notes to financial statements; and to prepare for the necessary accounting entries on the understatement of depreciation expense.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to determine the validity of the dormant payables and if found to have no legitimate claim against the Municipality, cause the reversion of the same to the unappropriated surplus; ensure that accounts payable are only recorded when the obligation is founded on a valid claim that is properly supported by sufficient evidence; and to adjust the balance of recorded obligations after full payment to unobligated allotment and the same shall be reconcile with the records of the Municipal Budget Officer; and
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to exert more effort in coordinating with the employees, contractors and suppliers and send a written notice informing them of their unclaimed payables.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to consider establishing cut-off hours at their assigned stations; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to direct all Collecting Officers to update their cashbooks daily and prepare the Report of Collections and Remittances/Deposits at the close of each business day.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that only permanent official shall handle the accountable forms; and
Instruct the Accountable Officer to refrain from recording unissued cash tickets as issued on the cashbook.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to comply with IPSAS's disclosure requirements by adequately providing a concise narrative on significant changes, a breakdown of material balances, and PPE reconciliation report to ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Human Resource Officer to submit the contracts of the job orders notwithstanding being attached to the DVs; and
Require the OIC-Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and Human Resource Officer to provide written justifications for the improper charging of the JO wagers under Personnel Services.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the MPDC to provide specific target dates of implementation of the PPAs and the related target schedule for each procurement activity in order to ensure timely implementation of projects; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the projects and at the same time respond to the causes of delays based on the actual implementation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Local School Board to furnish copies of the SEF budget and supplemental budgets, if any, to the Municipal Budget Office, Municipal Accounting Office and Municipal Treasurer’s Office within the prescribed period; and
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit quarterly and annual reports using the SEF Budget Accountability Form No. 1 (Annex B) on SEF utilization to the DepEd, copy furnished to the local sanggunian concerned, DepEd and Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that SEF disbursements are approved by proper officials.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the MDRRMO to fast-track the implementation of PPAs; and
Require the MDRRMO to ensure that PPAs of LDRRMF are fully implemented at the end of the year in order to strengthen its disaster preparedness.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to direct the MDRRMO to provide specific target dates of implementation of projects, programs, and activities; to review and assess the unimplemented projects and consider the possibility of proposing and re-appropriation to other priority projects, if warranted; and to conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the projects and at the same time respond to the causes of delays in the actual implementation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management to implement the following courses of actions:

No cash advances shall be granted to officers and employees except for travel purposes only.  Payment for meals, supplies and materials needed for the implementation of a project should be paid directly to the supplier and not thru the cash advances granted to officers and employees.  If payment in cash can’t be avoided, it is the responsibility of the Municipal Treasurer to do so.

Discontinue the practice of granting another cash advance to officers and employees with partially or unsettled cash advances pursuant to Section 89 of PD 1445.

The Municipal Accountant to send notice of demand to concerned officials and employees to liquidate their outstanding cash advances within the period provided under EO No. 298. After the lapse of the grace period, the Municipal Accountant shall provide the list of officials and employees to the Municipal Mayor for the possible imposition of the sanctions as prescribed in Section 14, EO 298.

Immediately cause the suspension of salaries of employees still connected with the agency until cash advances from previous years were fully liquidated.

Exert more efforts to locate documents that will serve as evidence in order to settle the accounts of the unliquidated cash advances.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Inventory Committee to prepare the required Physical Inventory Plan to be reviewed and approved by the LCE and submitted to the Audit Team at least 10 calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory-taking activities; and
Conduct the physical count efficiently and without delay following the PIP, and submit the required reports such as the RPCPPE, ICF, List of PPEs found at the Station, and List of Non-existing/Missing PPEs within the designated period.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the implementing department (e.g. Engineering Office) to identify, determine and reconcile those projects that were already completed and close the same to the appropriate PPE account;
Require the Engineering Office to provide the Accounting Office with copies of a comprehensive report of completed projects and other necessary documents; and
Based on the adjusted data, require the Municipal Accountant to compute for the required depreciation on the completed projects, and accordingly adjust the balances of the affected accounts.

We have recommended the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to review the accounts, identify/trace the noted unreconciled variance and prepare the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books upon verification.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Formulate a well-defined policy and guidelines on donation/financial assistance containing but not limited to the kind of financial assistance (medical, educational, funeral, etc.), approved amount per specific case, including the frequency of grants to each individual, and criteria in granting the donation and the documentary requirements for each particular case;
Medical and Funeral Assistance exceeding ₱10,000.00 should be referred to MSWDO for eligibility evaluation and the case study report should form part of the supporting documents for the claim, and the grant of assistance should be provided through a Guarantee Letter; and
Ensure that future grants are duly supported with the applicable requirements set forth under DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 15 Series of 2022.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Engineer to explain the discrepancies noted to avoid the issuance of a Notice of Disallowance and to be meticulous in the preparation of ABC and POW of the projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare a report of lacking DVs, cumulative and updated, every month and ensure that relevant information such as check number, JEV number, amount, payee, and nature of the transaction is provided in the report whenever incomplete submission of monthly DVs cannot be avoided; and
Submit the lacking DVs for CY 2023 together with all its supporting documents for audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare and submit regularly the Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPBB and Commission on Audit (COA) at the end of each semester and to observe the same in the years thereafter.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Ensure that the utilization of 20% Development Fund must be for capital expenditure and/or in the nature of investments; and
Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to stop charging expenditures against the 20% Development Fund for non-development programs and projects as enumerated under Section 3.2.5 of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Revisit the plan of the floating pontoon blocks for the marine protected area use and determine whether there is a need to augment the project to serve its intended purpose; and
Prioritize the utilization of 20% of the Development Fund for those projects of high importance essential to the promotion of the general welfare of the people and to attain the desired socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO and MBO to refrain from using the LDRRMF to pay administrative costs of the office to provide sufficient funds for the LDRRM programs.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDRRMO to provide specific target dates of implementation of projects, programs, and activities; to review and assess the unimplemented projects and consider the possibility of proposing and re-appropriation to other priority projects, if warranted; and to conduct regular monitoring and evaluation of the projects and at the same time respond to the causes of delays in the actual implementation.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to assign personnel who will consolidate and prepare the report, to propose a timeline for the submission of response from each department/office concerned of the audit recommendations, and to ensure adherence to the prescribed period for the submission of the AAPSI as provided under the GAA.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to:
Restrict access to cash accountable forms and their records to authorized personnel only to reduce the risk of unauthorized use and/or loss to the government.
Strictly implement the procedures on the requisitions of accountable forms and daily turnover of collections to strengthen the Municipality’s control over collections.
See to it that all collectors are properly supervised and their accountabilities are regularly monitored.
Require the erring accountable officer to remit the collections, and record, update and submit the cashbooks, RCD/RCR to COA, if issued; to present the missing accountable forms to COA, if unissued; or to give notices of loss in accordance of COA Circular no. 84-233 dated August 21, 1984.

We have recommended to the Municipal Treasurer to:
Strengthen its internal control over cash and accountable forms, identify its weaknesses, and monitor the implementation of stricter internal control on cash transactions.

We recommended the field collecting officer should remit his collections intact daily or in a regular basis to the Municipal Treasurer, who should in turn deposit in the authorized government depository bank immediately or as often as necessary to forestall losses through theft or misappropriation; and to stop the erroneous recording of the remittances in his cashbook.

Likewise, we recommended the Municipal Treasurer to strictly require the field collector to remit his collections intact daily or the next working day. We recommended:

1.	The Accounting Unit shall submit the Report of Collections and Deposits with copies of ORs and validated deposit slips, and the Report of Disbursements to the COA within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month, pursuant to Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
2.	The Municipal Treasurer may designate liquidating officer from among the accountable officers, who shall be in-charge of the following:
a.	Perform the procedures for the receipt and verification of collections turned over to him/her.
b.	Accomplish the RCD in four copies to summarize the collections turned over to him/her by the collectors as well as his/her own collections.
c.	Turn over intact the cash collections to the Municipal Treasurer together with the originals and two copies of the RCDs of collectors and the duplicates of the official receipts issued. The Municipal Treasurer shall in turn acknowledge receipt of the cash and all accompanying documents by signing all copies of the RCD of liquidating officer on the certification and receipt portion of the form.  The fourth copy of the RCD of the liquidating officer and RCDs of collectors/tellers shall be retained by the liquidating officer. Thereafter, the Municipal Treasurer shall deposit intact all collections turned over to her by the liquidating officer with the authorized depository bank daily or not later than the next banking day.  She shall record all deposits made in the cashbook and prepare the RCD, which shall be duly forwarded to the Accounting Unit.

The agency committed to comply with the above provision of rules and regulations. Towards this, the team shall undertake periodic monitoring.

Recommendation:
We have recommended to the Municipal Vice Mayor should apply for a fidelity bond, approved the disbursement vouchers for the appropriation of the sanggunian only and shall be allowed to countersigned in the check drawn.
The agency committed to comply with the above provision of rules and regulations. Towards this, the team shall undertake periodic monitoring.

a. The Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry for the stale checks; prepare a letter immediately to the concerned bank for the discrepancies found in the bank statements; maintain Subsidiary Ledger each collectors; and prioritize the verification of other recurring reconciling items to bring the cash in bank account in its correct balance.















b. The Municipal Treasurer to reconcile her cashbooks entries with that from the accounting records at least quarterly.



c. The Collecting Officers to submit the Report of Collections and Deposits to the accounting division individually

We recommended and management agreed that -      a. The Municipal Treasurer to initiate the closure of these dormant accounts to avoid further loss due to dormancy fees.                           

b. The Municipal Accountant to analyze the dormant accounts and return any excess of the project cost to the source/funding agency if warranted otherwise return these funds to the General Fund.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit her collections intact and daily to the authorized depository bank on the frequency required under existing regulation and to record her collections daily in the official cashbook.

We recommended and management agreed to require the Accountable Officers to apply with the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover their cash accountability and determine the maximum cash accountability to be assigned before the application with the Bureau of Treasury.
We recommended that management:      Discontinue the practice of granting new cash advances to officers/employees with unliquidated cash advances.

We recommended and management agreed to:  a. Discontinue granting of cash advances to Department of Education officials / employees. 

b.Send demand letter for the unliquidated cash advances to the Department of Education official and same should be furnished to the Department of Education Head of Agency and Accounting Office.

We recommended and management agreed to:  a. Direct the General Service Officer to submit the inventory reports of CY 2014 and CY 2015 and to prepare a timetable for the conduct of Physical Inventory and coordinate with the Inventory Committee members for a cohesive planning.

b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare necessary adjustments on the records deficiencies noted in the course of inventory taking and in the reconciliation of inventory report with the subsidiary ledgers and to prepare and maintain a subsidiary ledger card and property, plant and equipment ledger card.

We recommended to instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries in order to transfer the Property, Plant and Equipment account from Trust Fund books to the General Fund books and to compute for the depreciation expense accruing to these assets including those in prior years.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Journal Entry Voucher for the transfer of the unexpended balance of LDRMF fund to the Special Trust Fund crediting Trust Liability-DRRM under the Trust Fund and debiting Due to Other Funds under the General Fund.

We recommended and management agreed to:     a. Augment the work force assigned in the Accounting Office to facilitate timely recording and preparation of reports.

b. Direct the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer and other concerned employees to strictly adhere with the required time frame for the submission of accounts and financial reports.

We recommended and management concurred to direct the General Service Office/Procuring Officer to properly accomplish the Purchase Orders indicating all the vital information before it will be issued to the supplier.

We have recommended that management to implement the following courses of actions:

No cash advances shall be granted to officers and employees except for travel purposes only.  Payment for meals, supplies and materials needed for the implementation of a project should be paid directly to the supplier and not thru the cash advances granted to officers and employees.  If payment in cash can’t be avoided, it is the responsibility of the Municipal Treasurer to do so.

Discontinue the practice of granting another cash advances to officers and employees with partially or unsettled cash advances pursuant to Section 89 of PD 1445.

The Municipal Accountant to send notice of demand to concerned officials and employees to liquidate their outstanding cash advances within the period provided under EO No. 298. After the lapse of the grace period, the Municipal Accountant shall provide the list of officials and employees to the Municipal Mayor for the possible imposition of the sanctions as prescribed in Section 14, EO 298.

Immediately cause the suspension of salaries of employees still connected with the agency until cash advances from previous years were fully liquidated.

Exert more efforts to locate documents that will serve as evidence in order to settle the accounts of Hon. Macas.
observations.managementAction: Documents are still being retrieved in the case of the cash advances of the Late Hon. Macas.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the General Services Officer to comply strictly with the preparation of required reports enumerated in Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We have recommended to the GAD Focal person to ensure that activities identified as GAD are in conformity with the provisions under Philippine Commission on Women (PCW), NEDA and DBM Joint Circular No. 12-01 that is to respect, protect and fulfill the rights of woman at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres.

We have recommended  to the Human Resource Officer to review the appointment on the salary grade of Mrs. Umel and refer the matter to the Civil Service Commission for clarification.

We have also recommended to the Management to adhere strictly Section 4(d) of LBC no. 53 of the DBM guidelines to prevent improper disbursements that might result to disallowance in audit.

Require the concerned official to refund the amount collected since November 2006, the approval of her appointment up to the present.
